stories.summary: US has carried out more attacks on Iran, with Iranian media reporting explosions in Kish, Qeshm and Bandar Abbas.

stories.headline: China’s Crude Oil Imports Plunge to Lowest in Nearly a Decade
stories.summary: Chinese crude oil imports fell sharply in June to near a decade low, hampered by war in the Persian Gulf and an abrupt slowdown in domestic demand.

stories.headline: Maine ICE Shooting: What We Know About the Death of Joan Sebastian Guerrero
stories.summary: A federal immigration agent shot and killed a man in a car on Monday morning in Biddeford, Maine. It was the second fatal encounter in a week involving an agent and a person in a vehicle.

stories.headline: Yuan Still Cheap Versus Euro Even After Gain, Deutsche Bank Says
stories.summary: China’s yuan is still undervalued versus the euro despite its recent gains, and that’s contributing to a wider European Union trade deficit, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

stories.headline: Taiwan Fund Profits 80% After Intervening to Blunt Trump Tariffs
stories.summary: Taiwan reported a roughly 80% profit from a nine-month-long intervention to support the island’s stock market following the Trump administration’s move to impose tariffs, though it has now exited its position.

stories.headline: SK Hynix Selloff Deepens in Seoul After Wall Street Memory Slump
stories.summary: SK Hynix Inc. shares fell in Seoul after a rout on Wall Street, reflecting heightened caution among investors following this year’s unprecedented rally in global chipmaker stocks.

stories.headline: SocGen Sees $76 Billion of JGB Buying If GPIF Rebalances Assets
stories.summary: Japan’s Government Pension Investment Fund has room to buy as much as ¥12.3 trillion ($76 billion) of additional government bonds without changing its asset allocation mix, according to Societe Generale SA, offering potential support for the debt market.

stories.headline: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.headline: Iran military warns US against Strait of Hormuz ‘interference’

stories.headline: British couple named among 13 killed in Spanish wildfires

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to serve the rest of his Senate term

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s legacy: Israel advocate, Trump ally, Iran war supporter
stories.summary: Late US senator backed invasion of Iraq, war on Iran and approach of 'more for Israel' as he became a Trump confidant.

stories.headline: Trump reimposes US blockade and demands 20% Hormuz shipping fee

stories.headline: Kyiv attacked after Ukraine launches coalition to tackle Russia missiles

stories.headline: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office

stories.headline: Volkswagen planning to cut up to 100,000 jobs globally

stories.headline: Indian activist on hunger strike at 'cockroach' protest loses 8.2kg

stories.summary: The private equity owner of Rawlings Sporting Goods Co., one of the oldest makers of baseball apparel and equipment, is exploring the sale of its stake in the company in an effort to tap booming investor demand for sports-related investments.

stories.headline: Shein’s Slowing Growth Risks Weighing on Hong Kong IPO Valuation
stories.summary: Shein has finally won long-awaited Chinese regulatory approval for its planned Hong Kong listing, but slowing business growth threatens to weigh on the valuation it can command in the initial public offering.

Mike Mayo, Head of US Large-Cap Bank Research at Wells Fargo, joined the program to share his outlook.
